channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Scientists Tickled Gorillas And Found A 15-Million-Year-Old Clue To How Humans Learned Speech - Forbes
item.description:
1. Scientists Tickled Gorillas And Found A 15-Million-Year-Old Clue To How Humans Learned Speech  Forbes
  2. Rhythm and timing in laughter reveal that human vocal plasticity falls on a hominid continuum | Communications Biology  Nature
  3. To Reveal the Rhythmic Roots of Laughter, Just Tickle an Ape  The New York Times
  4. Great apes (including us) have been giggling for 15 million years  Popular Science
  5. Scientists Just Discovered How Long Humans Have Been Laughing, and It’s Way Longer Than You Think  VICE

  4. The strawberry moon will soon rise. When to look up.  The Washington Post
  5. ‘Strawberry Moon’: Here’s when to look up to see June’s full moon  FOX 4 News Dallas-Fort Worth

item.title: NASA is paying $30 million for a 1st-of-its-kind rescue mission to the aging Swift telescope before it falls from space. Is it worth it? - Space
item.description:
1. NASA is paying $30 million for a 1st-of-its-kind rescue mission to the aging Swift telescope before it falls from space. Is it worth it?  Space
  2. Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. 1st-of-its-kind mission will attempt to save aging space telescope using robot spacecraft  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  4. This Week, NASA Will Launch a First-of-Its-Kind Mission to Save a Space Telescope That Has Observed the Sky for Nearly 22 Years  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. Inside the Swift-Boost Mission: Saving a Telescope with a Flying Rocket Launcher  Yahoo

item.title: Science news this week: Life on Mars, weird water and a curious human cousin - Live Science
item.description:
1. Science news this week: Life on Mars, weird water and a curious human cousin  Live Science
  2. Nasa rover detects potential signatures of ancient microbial life on Mars  The Guardian
  3. A Mars rover found organic carbon just sitting on a rock  Science News
  4. In one drilled Martian rock, Curiosity found 21 organic molecules — seven never before detected on Mars — including a nitrogen-bearing ring structure that belongs to the same chemical family as precursors to RNA and DNA.  Space Daily
  5. Possible signs of ancient life on Mars are rich in complex carbon  New Scientist

item.title: Massive asteroid the size of multiple Empire State Buildings is zipping past Earth this weekend - New York Post
item.description:

  3. A large, harmless asteroid will zip past Earth this weekend  AP News
  4. Watch a 'Potentially hazardous asteroid' the size of a skyscraper close in on Earth live online tonight  Space
  5. Asteroid as large as 5 cruise ships will soar past Earth this weekend  Popular Science

item.title: Astronomers used to assume galaxies formed first and slowly grew the black holes at their centres. But JWST is now finding objects in the early universe where the black hole appears to have arrived first — already enormous before a full galaxy had grown aro - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Astronomers used to assume galaxies formed first and slowly grew the black holes at their centres. But JWST is now finding objects in the early universe where the black hole appears to have arrived first — already enormous before a full galaxy had grown aro  Space Daily
  2. James Webb Captures Strongest Evidence Yet for Black Hole Stars  DIYPhotography
  3. This mysterious “red dot” stuns scientists: a black hole before its galaxy?  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  4. Black Hole Too Big for Its Age Spotted by James Webb  tovima.com
  5. James Webb Uncovers the Nature of Mysterious "Little Red Dots" in the Universe  Zamin.uz

item.title: Boeing’s Starliner Is Such a Disaster That We Don’t Even Have Words - Futurism
item.description:

  4. Starliner Return To Flight Still About One Year Away  Aviation Week

item.title: Ghost particle could have traveled 11 billion light-years to reach Earth - CNN
item.description:
1. Ghost particle could have traveled 11 billion light-years to reach Earth  CNN

item.title: Black hole's 'point of no escape' studied with the loudest gravitational waves ever heard - Space
item.description:

  4. A ‘direct wave’ from colliding black holes reveals signature of a whirlpool in spacetime  The Conversation
  5. A New Way to Touch the Most Violent Gravity in the Universe  ScienceBlog.com

item.title: The Roman Space Telescope has the same size mirror as Hubble, but it will survey the sky up to 1,000 times faster — not because it sees deeper, but because it sees wider: each Roman image captures a patch of sky at least 100 times larger, making Hubble’s - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Roman Space Telescope has the same size mirror as Hubble, but it will survey the sky up to 1,000 times faster — not because it sees deeper, but because it sees wider: each Roman image captures a patch of sky at least 100 times larger, making Hubble’s  Space Daily
  2. Experience the Launch of NASA’s Roman Space Telescope  NASA (.gov)
  3. The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope Arrives in Florida Ahead of Launch  Universe Today
  4. NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida  Spaceflight Now
  5. NASA's Roman Space Telescope Arrives in Florida, New Treatment Could Relieve Knee Pain Without Surgery, and a Cave Full of All-Female Skeletons  Discover Magazine

item.title: SpaceX won a NASA contract worth up to $843 million to build a single machine whose only job is to drag the space station out of orbit and into the Pacific Ocean - Space Daily
item.description:
1. SpaceX won a NASA contract worth up to $843 million to build a single machine whose only job is to drag the space station out of orbit and into the Pacific Ocean  Space Daily

  4. Experts Concerned About Plan to Destroy International Space Station  Futurism

item.title: Scientists Think Uranus and Neptune May Not Be the Ice Giants We Imagined - Gizmodo
item.description:

  4. Gas from Uranus reveals it has an icy centre  New Scientist
  5. Ice Giants Revisited: Uranus And Neptune As Magma Ocean Worlds  Astrobiology Web

item.title: Look What NASA’s Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter Just Spotted Lurking in the Red Planet’s Arbot Hills - The Debrief

  5. Chinese UFO detected in orbit as space spy theories erupt  Daily Express

item.title: Scientists took one of the naked mole rat’s longevity tricks — a gene that helps it produce unusually protective hyaluronan — and put it into mice. The result: the mice lived longer, had less inflammation, and were more resistant to tumours. - Space Daily
item.description: Scientists took one of the naked mole rat’s longevity tricks — a gene that helps it produce unusually protective hyaluronan — and put it into mice. The result: the mice lived longer, had less inflammation, and were more resistant to tumours.  Space Daily

item.description: NASA’s PACE Mission Studies Smoke, Fires  NASA Science (.gov)

item.title: Only one spacecraft has ever visited Uranus or Neptune: Voyager 2, which flew past Uranus in 1986 and Neptune in 1989. Nearly four decades later, no mission has returned, leaving many of the biggest questions those flybys raised — about the planets’ interi - Space Daily
item.description: Only one spacecraft has ever visited Uranus or Neptune: Voyager 2, which flew past Uranus in 1986 and Neptune in 1989. Nearly four decades later, no mission has returned, leaving many of the biggest questions those flybys raised — about the planets’ interi  Space Daily

  3. Titanosaur Finds Unearthed at Lo Hueco, Cuenca  Mirage News

item.description: Clean crystal surface lets single molecules hit ultimate quantum limit  Phys.org

item.title: In 2018, low Earth orbit still had months of margin: if satellites stopped dodging one another, researchers estimate it could take 164 days before a catastrophic collision. By 2025, after the rise of megaconstellations, that margin had collapsed to just 5.5 days. - Space Daily
item.description: In 2018, low Earth orbit still had months of margin: if satellites stopped dodging one another, researchers estimate it could take 164 days before a catastrophic collision. By 2025, after the rise of megaconstellations, that margin had collapsed to just 5.5 days.  Space Daily

item.title: The Prehistoric Disaster That Almost Erased Humans From Earth - Mental Floss
item.description: The Prehistoric Disaster That Almost Erased Humans From Earth  Mental Floss

item.title: A bowhead whale can live for more than 200 years, yet it appears unusually resistant to cancer — and its genome carries genetic changes that may help explain why. Researchers have found signs of enhanced DNA repair in bowhead cells, turning one of the - Space Daily
item.description: A bowhead whale can live for more than 200 years, yet it appears unusually resistant to cancer — and its genome carries genetic changes that may help explain why. Researchers have found signs of enhanced DNA repair in bowhead cells, turning one of the  Space Daily

item.title: In 1977, Ann Druyan recorded an hour of her brainwaves and heartbeat just two days after she and Carl Sagan agreed to marry, and NASA pressed that compressed minute onto Voyager's Golden Record as a private love letter now more than 25 billion kilome - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In 1977, Ann Druyan recorded an hour of her brainwaves and heartbeat just two days after she and Carl Sagan agreed to marry, and NASA pressed that compressed minute onto Voyager's Golden Record as a private love letter now more than 25 billion kilome  Space Daily
  2. Whoever finds the Voyager Golden Record will know exactly how long it has been drifting, because the scientists who built it pressed a small sample of uranium into the cover — a built-in clock that keeps time for roughly a billion years.  Space Daily

item.title: Soviet spacecraft built to land on Venus in 1972 spent 53 years orbiting Earth and finally returned witho - The Times of India
item.description:
1. Soviet spacecraft built to land on Venus in 1972 spent 53 years orbiting Earth and finally returned witho  The Times of India
  2. In 1970, the Soviet Venera 7 probe survived 23 minutes on the surface of Venus before the 475-degree heat and 90-atmosphere pressure crushed it, but in those minutes it became the first human-made object to transmit data from another planet, and engineer  Space Daily
  3. In 1982 a Soviet Probe Landed on Venus and Recorded the First Sounds Ever Heard From Another Planet, Before the Heat Cooked It to Death  19FortyFive
  4. The Soviet spacecraft landed on Venus and measured its own cover instead of the planet's surface  UA.NEWS
  5. The Soviet Union launched nearly 30 spacecraft at Venus and became the only nation ever to land on its surface and send back pictures — color photographs of a scorched volcanic plain under a yellow-green sky, shot in the few dozen minutes before the heat  19FortyFive

item.description:

  4. Scientists believed they understood what might be at the center of our galaxy. However, they might have been entirely mistaken the entire time.  vocal.media

item.title: The James Webb Space Telescope found a spiral galaxy, nicknamed the Big Wheel, that existed just two billion years after the Big Bang — five times more massive than the Milky Way, with a spiral structure our models say couldn't have survived that early - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The James Webb Space Telescope found a spiral galaxy, nicknamed the Big Wheel, that existed just two billion years after the Big Bang — five times more massive than the Milky Way, with a spiral structure our models say couldn't have survived that early  Space Daily
  2. James Webb Space Telescope catches 6 galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe  Space
  3. The JWST Spies Six Galaxies Becoming One  Universe Today
  4. JWST found a galaxy cluster 10 billion years old that shouldn’t be this dense  Martin Cid Magazine
  5. 🔭 James Webb discovers unexpected 'naked' black hole  Techno-Science.net
